hypothesis_raw: Good morning, Mr. Johnson. What brings you in today? I've been having this pressure in my chest. It started about two hours ago. Can you describe what it feels like? Like something heavy sitting on my chest. Dull, not sharp. Does it radiate anywhere, your arm, jaw back? A little into my left arm, and up to my jaw. On a scale of 1 to 10, how bad is it right now? Around a 6. It was worse when I was walking to the car. Anything make it better or worse? It got better when I sat down. Worse with walking. Any shortness of breath, sweating, or nausea? I felt sweaty at first. A little short of breath, too. You said it started two hours ago. Was this the first episode, or have you felt it before? Actually, now that I think about it, I had something similar yesterday evening after dinner. It went away in a few minutes, so I didn't mention it. Got it any history of heart disease, high blood pressure, or diabetes? High blood pressure for about 10 years. My father had a heart attack at 58. What medications are you on? Amlidipine for my blood pressure, adorvastatin for cholesterol, and a daily aspirin. Any smoking or alcohol? I smoked a pack a day for 30 years, quit 5 years ago. I want to get an ECG and a troponin and apply the heart score to assess your risk.

hypothesis_raw: Good afternoon, Ms. Smith. Tell me what brought you in today. I've had this pain in my belly since last night. It's getting worse. Where exactly is the pain? Can you point to it? It started around my belly button, but now it's more on the lower right side. On a scale of 1 to 10, how bad is it right now? Maybe a 7. It's sharp, and it hurts more when I move or cough. Any nausea, vomiting, or fever? I threw up once this morning. I feel warm, but I didn't check my temperature. You said it started last night. What time exactly? Actually, thinking back, it was more like yesterday afternoon around 3, not last night. Understood. Any change in appetite or bowel movements? I haven't wanted to eat anything. No diarrhea. Any medications you take regularly? Just ibuprofen for headaches and a multivitamin. Nothing prescription. Any prior surgeries? No, never. I'm going to examine your abdomen now. I'll press gently. Tell me where it hurts most.

hypothesis_raw: Hello, Mr. Smith. What's going on today? I've been short of breath. It came on pretty suddenly. When did it start? This morning, maybe around eight. I was just sitting at my desk. Any chest pain with it? A little. It's sharp, and it's worse when I take a deep breath. Any cough, fever, or leg swelling? No cough, no fever. But my right calf has been sore for a few days. days. Have you traveled recently or been immobile for long periods? I flew back from Europe last week. It was a long flight, maybe nine hours. You said it came on this morning. Was it really sudden or did it build up? Actually, now that I think about it, I noticed it a bit yesterday evening too. It just got much worse this morning. Without it, any history of asthma, heart problems, or clots? No asthma. My father had a DVT, I think. Medications. I take lisinopril for blood pressure, and I'm on a daily aspirin. Any smoking history? I quit about 10 years ago. Smoked for 20 years before that. I want to check your oxygen saturation and listen to your lungs.

hypothesis_raw: Hi, Ms. Smith. What brings you in today? I've got a really bad headache. I've never had one like this. When did it start? About two hours ago. It hit me all at once, like something exploded in my head. Where is the pain? All over, but mostly the back of my head, and my neck feels stiff. On a scale of 1 to 10? A 10. It's the worst headache of my life. life any nausea vomiting or vision changes I feel sick to my stomach and bright light really bothers me any weakness numbness or trouble speaking no nothing like that you said this started two hours ago was it sudden or gradual actually it's been closer to an hour I was trying to wait it out before coming in okay any history of migraines I get headaches sometimes but they're never like this this is different any medications I took to acetaminophen about 30 minutes ago no relief I'm also on suma triptan for my usual migraines but I haven't taken it today any family history of aneurysm or stroke my aunt had a brain bleed in her 50s. I'm going to examine your neck and do a neurologic exam now.

reference: Hi, Ms. Smith. What brings you in today? I've got a really bad headache. I've never had one like this. When did it start? About two hours ago. It hit me all at once, like something exploded in my head. Where is the pain? All over, but mostly the back of my head and my neck feels stiff. On a scale of one to ten? A ten. It's the worst headache of my life. Any nausea, vomiting, or vision changes? I feel sick to my stomach, and bright light really bothers me. Any weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking? No, nothing like that. You said this started two hours ago — was it sudden or gradual? Actually, it's been closer to an hour. I was trying to wait it out before coming in. Okay. Any history of migraines? I get headaches sometimes, but they're never like this. This is different. Any medications? I took two acetaminophen about thirty minutes ago. No relief. I'm also on sumatriptan for my usual migraines, but I haven't taken it today. Any family history of aneurysm or stroke? My aunt had a brain bleed in her fifties. I'm going to examine your neck and do a neurologic exam now.

hypothesis_raw: Good morning, Mr. Smith. What brings you in? I've been really tired for months now, and I think I'm losing weight without trying. How much weight and over what period? Maybe 15 pounds over the last three months. My clothes are loose. Any change in appetite? Actually, my appetite is okay. Sometimes I feel hungrier than usual, but the weight keeps coming off. Any night sweats or fevers? I wake up damp sometimes, yeah. Not every night. How's your mood been, sleep? Sleep is okay. Mood, I guess I've been down, but I think it's because I feel so tired all the time. You said 15 pounds. Are you sure about that number? Let me think. It might be closer to 20. I was 185, and I weighed myself this morning at 165. Any bowel changes, blood and stool, or urinary changes? I go to the bathroom more than I used to. I'm thirsty all the time. Any lumps or a history of cancer in the family? No lumps I've noticed. My mother had breast cancer at 60. I take Metformin for prediabetes and Levothyroxine for my thyroid. I'd like to get some labs, a CBC, TSH, metabolic panel, and hemoglobin A1c.

I felt lightheaded, a bit sweaty, and my vision went gray around the edges. How long were you out? My husband said maybe ten seconds. I came to quickly. any chest pain palpitations or shortness of breath before it happened no chest pain my heart felt like it was racing for a second but it was quick did you bite your tongue or lose bladder control no nothing like that you said your heart was racing was that before or after you felt lightheaded actually thinking about it the racing came first then the lightheaded feeling then I went down okay that's important any similar episodes before I felt dizzy when I stand up quickly but I've never passed out before any recent illness fluid loss or changes in medication I had a stomach bug two days ago some diarrhea I haven't been drinking much water. What medications do you take? I'm on metoprolol and hydrochlorothiazide for blood pressure and a baby aspirin. I'd like to check orthostatic vitals and get an EKG.

reference: Hello, Ms. Smith. Tell me what happened. I passed out this morning. I was standing in the kitchen and the next thing I knew I was on the floor. Any warning before you passed out? I felt lightheaded, a bit sweaty, and my vision went gray around the edges. How long were you out? My husband said maybe ten seconds. I came to quickly. Any chest pain, palpitations, or shortness of breath before it happened? No chest pain. My heart felt like it was racing for a second, but it was quick. Did you bite your tongue or lose bladder control? No, nothing like that. You said your heart was racing — was that before or after you felt lightheaded? Actually, thinking about it, the racing came first. Then the lightheaded feeling, then I went down. Okay, that's important. Any similar episodes before? I've felt dizzy when I stand up quickly, but I've never passed out before. Any recent illness, fluid loss, or changes in medication? I had a stomach bug two days ago, some diarrhea. I haven't been drinking much water. What medications do you take? I'm on metoprolol and hydrochlorothiazide for blood pressure. And a baby aspirin. I'd like to check orthostatic vitals and get an EKG.
